destinations.evidence: Canonical commitment processing computes a required expiry block from commit_block + ttl and stores it in async metadata.
The pool has a non-optional contract_expiry_block, uses it for eligibility and cleanup, and extends it to the Phase 2 deadline on AwaitingDelivery transition.
destinations.snippet:
pub contract_expiry_block: u64
...
metadata.state == AsyncState::Fulfilled && current_block <= metadata.contract_expiry_block
...
(current_block > metadata.contract_expiry_block).then_some(*hash)
...
if phase2_deadline > metadata.contract_expiry_block { metadata.contract_expiry_block = phase2_deadline; }

equivalence_argument: The current pool makes expiry required by type rather than Option. The commitment-finalization path computes it from contract TTL, fulfilled/delivery-ready selection and cleanup use only contract_expiry_block for block expiry, and Phase 1 settlement extends the boundary to the computed Phase 2 deadline when applicable.
rationale: The merge adds contract_expiry_block to AsyncTxMeta, requires expiry_block when adding transactions, removes fallback TTL calculations, treats missing expiry as a critical invariant violation, and updates the expiry to max_poll_block during Phase 2 transition to match contract markPhase1Settled behavior. This result keeps the candidate as a distinct refined invariant because the bounded assignment packet does not contain a newer candidate that clearly supersedes it. Implementation-detail caveat: The metadata field name is internal, but aligning pool retention with AsyncJobTracker expiry is observable in which async jobs remain eligible for settlement/delivery.
statement: Async pool entries should carry a required contract_expiry_block set at commitment finalization, use it as the sole block-based expiry source, and update it to the Phase 2 deadline when a two-phase job transitions to AwaitingDelivery.
suggested_falsification_tests: Add an async pool entry with expiry block 120 and assert it expires only when current_block > 120; transition a two-phase entry with max_poll_block 200 to AwaitingDelivery and assert its block expiry becomes 200; construct a malformed meta with None expiry and assert it is treated as expired.
summary: Async pool expiry is driven by required contract expiry metadata and Phase 2 extension.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Add an async pool entry with expiry block 120 and assert it expires only when current_block > 120; transition a two-phase entry with max_poll_block 200 to AwaitingDelivery and assert its block expiry becomes 200; construct a malformed meta with None expiry and assert it is treated as expired.
title: Async pool expiry follows contract expiry
